text:
Once upon a sunny Saturday, a woman named Clara decided to embark on an impromptu road trip. Armed with a weather-appropriate outfit, a comfortable pair of shoes, and determination, Clara hit the road. Her destination was unclear; she had merely dreamed of wanderlust.

Her journey started at her cozy town library. The librarian, enchanted by Clara's story-seeking mood, suggested a couple of books on historical expeditions.

First, Clara found ‘Exploring America: A History,’ a big, intriguing tome. From it, she learned about Lewis Carroll, who spent parts of his adolescence exploring America. Next, she picked up ‘Across the Continent.’ Both books laid out the paths taken and adventures of early explorers.

After hours of reading, Clara felt wanderlust creeping in. She made her way to the park nearby.

There she spotted a group of nature enthusiasts on a trail. They had compasses dangling from their belts and were scanning the landscape. Clara joined them.
